title: ranunculus spicatus subsp. cortusifolius
intro: Ranunculus spicatus subsp. cortusifolius, also known as the prickly buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus spicatus plant that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant contains alkaloids and glycosides, which are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus spicatus subsp. cortusifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of tinctures, infusions, or decoctions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
